content: is there a lint for rustdoc to check if there is a type written in backtics that could be turned into a lint?
if not, I think it would be useful to have
This is probably about the closest thing https://rust-lang.github.io/rust-clippy/master/index.html#/doc_markdown

that could be turned into a lint?

You mean intra-doc links, right?

Yeah, I agree it would be useful, even if it has false positives and cannot be enabled all the time. In fact, one that would check even words outside backticks to see if they are types could also be useful.
I was coming here to ask the same.Can we enable this one but only for words in backticks?

I was referring to the doc_markdown lint, but upon further reading it does not actually do what I thought it does. What I was looking for is a lint that would find text in backticks that are actually type names, and warn that they are not linked.
maybe that could be done in some script?, or create a script that do that? if not linter is doing that. Or in checkpatch.pl?
I'd rather have it directly in rust doc

Andreas Hindborg said:

I was referring to the doc_markdown lint, but upon further reading it does not actually do what I thought it does. What I was looking for is a lint that would find text in backticks that are actually type names, and warn that they are not linked.

Yeah, that is what Benno was asking for, as far as I understand, but apparently there is no such lint.

I would also like one that warns for those that are outside backticks too (possibly opt-in), since we also have those cases and would make people at least check (or we can tell them about it in review).

I have asked the rustdoc maintainer about it.
